prose:
Method provenance:
Property: HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans) gap, in eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) using PySCF 2.x
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied aniline.xyz (no further optimisation). 14 atoms.
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
SCF convergence: converged in 9 cycles (energy and gradient thresholds met)
Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine
Live log: the PySCF output was streamed to the live `.out` file created by chemkit during execution (path printed at launch; can be inspected with `tail -f`).

Result:
HOMO = –5.553772 eV
LUMO = –0.2489544 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV
Vertical ionisation potential (Koopmans) = 5.553772 eV
Vertical electron affinity (Koopmans) = 0.2489544 eV

The calculation employed the chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill with the settings above; the headline gap value reproduced here is exactly as returned by the tool.
